description: Compute between-class vs. within-class TRV variance + gradient norm correlation using existing 3-seed probe data. ANOVA: class label + gradient norm -> TRV. If >80% of variance is explained, per-test-point diagnostics are fundamentally flawed (Contrarian H-Con1). This is the GATE experiment for all further investment — it determines whether per-test-point attribution sensitivity exists as a data property or is just class membership plus noise.
blocks: All rescue angles, all further planning

action: Complete TRV-high vs. TRV-low LDS comparison (missing Probe Step 8)
description: Pre-registered but never executed. Split test points into TRV-high (Level >= 3) and TRV-low (Level 1) subsets. Compute per-test-point LDS using Full GGN attribution as approximate ground truth. Report Cohen's d with 95% CI. If d < 0.3, TRV has no demonstrated link to attribution quality — 'stable' may mean 'stably wrong.' This blocks all claims about TRV's practical utility.
effort: ~30 min analysis on existing data (approximate via Full GGN as reference)

action: Compute softmax confidence vs. TRV correlation
description: ~10 lines of code on existing data. If prediction entropy or softmax confidence explains TRV variance (Spearman |rho| > 0.5), TRV adds nothing beyond confidence thresholding. The Codex reviewer (5/10) specifically flagged this missing trivial baseline.

action: Compute cross-seed attribution variance vs. TRV correlation (Daunce differentiation)
description: Using existing 3-seed data, compute per-test-point cross-seed attribution variance (analogous to Daunce's model perturbation signal) and correlate with TRV. If Spearman rho > 0.7, TRV and Daunce are measuring the same thing — AURA's independent novelty collapses.

action: Define hard kill switch with concrete criteria
description: Specify: 'If [class ANOVA explains >80% of TRV variance] AND [BSS cross-seed rho < 0.3] AND [Disagreement Diagnostic quality-prediction rho < 0.2], abandon per-test-point diagnostics entirely.' Without this, the project risks infinite rescue attempts. Also specify the fallback: negative result paper targeting NeurIPS D&B track or TMLR.

action: Force convergence to ONE rescue angle after diagnostics complete
description: After priorities 1-4 are completed, if per-test-point signal survives, pick exactly ONE rescue angle. Recommendation: Pragmatist Angle 1 (Disagreement Diagnostic) — lowest compute cost (~3.5h), no theoretical dependencies, most direct empirical test. Gate ALL other angles on this result.

The most robust Probe finding: ~20% of test points are immune to Hessian approximation choice. Characterize: class distribution, representation-space centroid distance, gradient norm, loss value, prediction confidence. Test whether a simple logistic classifier can predict TRV=5 with AUROC > 0.75. This could be a cleaner contribution than rescuing the full TRV framework.

action: Scale probe to 500 test points with confidence intervals
description: N=100 is underpowered for all correlation and distribution claims. Scale to 500 uniform-random test points. Report 95% bootstrap CIs for all correlations, distribution percentages, and effect sizes.

State clearly: (a) what the project CAN claim (Hessian choice reshapes attributions; immune subset exists), (b) what it CANNOT claim (stable per-test-point TRV, SI proxy, adaptive fusion superiority), (c) what the open question is (data property vs. model-instance noise). All subsequent work anchored to this.

action: Develop concrete fallback paper plan (negative result / empirical analysis)
description: Outline the Contrarian's negative result paper: title, abstract, 4 key claims, required experiments (ANOVA across 3 datasets/architectures, Pareto frontier, stability-correctness analysis), target venue (NeurIPS D&B, TMLR). Budget: ~80 GPU-hours. This should be developed IN PARALLEL with rescue angle validation.

action: Update contribution tracker with honest status
description: C0: 'FAILED critical pre-registered criterion; requires fundamental redefinition.' C1: 'Blocked by C0.' C2: 'Abandoned — H4 falsified.' C3: 'Blocked by C0.' C4: 'Blocked by missing TRV-LDS experiment (Step 8).'

action: Test continuous TRV (damped GGN with varying lambda)
description: Replace discrete hierarchy with continuous damping (lambda from 0.001 to 100, 20 steps) on existing last-layer setup. If continuous TRV has more per-point variance than discrete TRV, the hierarchy collapse issue can be mitigated without full-model Hessian.

action: Test Jaccard@50 and weighted Kendall tau as alternative TRV metrics
description: Jaccard@10 is extremely sensitive to small score perturbations with 50K training points. If cross-seed stability improves with larger k or rank-weighted metrics, the original instability finding may be partially a metric artifact.
